# T1008 — Fallback Channels

**Tactics:** Command and Control  ·  **Platforms:** ESXi, Linux, macOS, Windows

## Description

Adversaries may use fallback or alternate communication channels if the primary channel is compromised or inaccessible in order to maintain reliable command and control and to avoid data transfer thresholds.

## Detection

**Behavioral Detection of Fallback or Alternate C2 Channels**
- Establishing network connections on uncommon ports or protocols following C2 disruption or blocking. Often executed by processes that typically exhibit no network activity. _(log sources: NSM:Flow, WinEventLog:Sysmon)_
- Creation of outbound connections on alternate ports or using covert transport (e.g., ICMP, DNS) from non-network-intensive processes, following known disruption or blocked traffic. _(log sources: NSM:Flow, auditd:SYSCALL)_
- Outbound fallback traffic from low-profile or background launch agents using unusual protocols or destinations after primary channel inactivity. _(log sources: NSM:Flow, macos:unifiedlog)_
- Outbound traffic from host management services or guest-to-host interactions over unusual interfaces (e.g., backdoor API endpoints or external VPN tunnels). _(log sources: esxi:vmkernel, esxi:vpxd)_

## Mitigations

- **Network Intrusion Prevention** (M1031): Use intrusion detection signatures to block traffic at network boundaries.

## Reference

- ATT&CK: https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1008
